title: Bridge Essay: Active inference as a theory of sentient behavior.
concept: Predictive processing — free energy principle, active inference, Bayesian brain.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Dependent Arising** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Dependent arising — predictive processing, free energy principle, active inference as causal cognition.
**Phase context:** Predictive processing — free energy principle, active inference, Bayesian brain.

**Active inference as a theory of sentient behavior.** — Biol Psychol (2024). DOI: 10.1016/j.biopsycho.2023.108741. PMID: 38182015. This review paper offers an overview of the history and future of active inference-a unifying perspective on action and perception. Active inference is based upon the idea that sentient behavior depends upon our brains' implicit use of internal models to predict, infer, and direct action. Our focus is upon the conceptual roots and development of this theory of (basic) sentience and does not follow a rigid chronological narrative.
